architecture_fit.note: Hand-built shift-and-score matched filter composes across positions and solves multi-token matching; a small mechanism but not a base_model.py attention block.

baseline_comparison.note: Uses the goal's linear (last-concrete-token) baseline; clearly beats it at L=3 (0.84 vs 0.44, lift +0.40) and across most lengths.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic, no model to ablate and the README never says so; worse, README claims a min/AND composition but the code actually sums similarities.

operating_range.note: Swept across the goal's composition-depth axis L=1..6 with graceful degradation; length_robustness 0.93, though FPR climbs 0.05->0.25 (consistent with summing not AND).

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire mechanism is analytically derived from pattern+embeddings with no training, reproducing the target behaviour.

visual_judgement.note: Demo per-position attention vs ground-truth plot with run/length/seed controls is legible, but the sweep-summary panel the README promises is absent from the app.

visualisation_rationale.note: Rationale ties the chart to length_robustness reasonably, but describes a summary sweep panel that is not actually implemented.

notes: A hand-built shift-and-accumulate matched filter that genuinely composes across positions, clearly beats the no-composition baseline at the canonical length, and holds sharpness across the L=1..6 sweep (length_robustness 0.93). It is dragged down by faithfulness gaps: the README claims an element-wise min/AND composition while the code sums similarities, and it never addresses the absence of a model-level causal check. The Demo viz is legible but omits the sweep-summary panel the README advertises.
